what_it_does: A self-contained acceptance test that regenerates the design blueprint and verifies it is complete, internally consistent, and idempotent: required blueprint files exist, built inventory entries map to real source files, every surface-spec address appears in addresses.json, every register.json view has a surface spec, inventory addresses cover the registered set exactly, each spec's home_journey matches its directory, and re-emitting the blueprint produces identical content.
observations: Runs blueprint_emit.py via subprocess as the first step and again for idempotency
Uses global PASS/FAILS counters and a check() helper that prints ok/XX lines
Compares sets to enforce coverage: view ids vs spec ids, inventory addresses vs registered addresses
Verifies README contains references to the lint gate and a prohibition on inventing addresses
Hashes only the BLUEPRINT directory for the idempotency snapshot, not _system or DESIGN root files
Reads register.json from the DESIGN root and addresses.json from _system
Treats component paths in inventory as relative to the repo root
standards_out: The B1 'proof bar' concept: completeness + consistency without a full frontend rebuild
The 'path-of-least-resistance law' check that forbids inventing addresses
The idempotency/reproducibility check for the generated blueprint
conventions: Top-level docstring explains scope, run command, and self-contained nature
Uppercase path constants at module top
Small check() helper with global mutable pass/fail state
Set algebra for completeness and consistency assertions
Loud failure via SystemExit(1) with a printed failure list
concerns: Global mutable PASS/FAILS state makes the test harder to reuse or parallelize
The idempotency snapshot only covers BLUEPRINT, so changes in _system or DESIGN root files written by blueprint_emit.py are not detected
Examples check uses os.listdir(ex_dir) and filters f.endswith('.md') but never confirms the entries are files
Files are opened without explicit encoding
JSON parse errors are not handled gracefully; they will bubble up as raw tracebacks
notes: This is the B1 acceptance gate: it proves the generated blueprint is complete, consistent, and reproducible before anything else trusts it.
questions: Should the idempotency snapshot include _system and DESIGN root files that blueprint_emit.py may also modify?
Should the examples check verify files rather than names, or should examples be directories containing .md files?
Should shared_components planned rows also be checked for missing component paths?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it checks blueprint completeness, consistency, and idempotency as described.
apparent_themes: Preventing drift between code, registry, and generated documentation
Idempotency and reproducibility of generated artifacts
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the blueprint a trustworthy, machine-verifiable contract between the design system and the codebase, where any mismatch or invented address is caught immediately.
novelty: It treats blueprint completeness as a set-theoretic problem—inventory, addresses, register views, and surface specs must all cover each other exactly—and adds a directory-structure consistency check for home_journey.
contribution: A concrete, runnable acceptance test that defines and enforces the completeness/consistency/idempotency contract for the design blueprint, giving downstream tooling a reliable signal that the blueprint matches reality.
summary_for_embedding: A self-contained Python acceptance test that verifies the design blueprint is complete, consistent, regenerable, and idempotent by checking required files, component inventory mappings, surface-spec address registration, view coverage, and directory placement.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a loud-failing B1 gate that proves the importable blueprint accurately reflects the real codebase and registered design addresses without inventing or omitting anything.

symbols.does: Records a pass or fail for a labeled assertion and prints an ok/XX line.
symbols.description: The central assertion helper used throughout the test. It increments the global PASS counter when the condition is true, otherwise appends the label and detail to FAILS and prints a failure marker.

symbols.does: Opens a file and returns its parsed JSON content.
symbols.description: A thin wrapper around json.load used to read addresses.json, register.json, component-inventory.json, and surface spec files.

symbols.does: Runs the full B1 blueprint completeness, consistency, and idempotency acceptance suite.
symbols.description: It regenerates the blueprint, checks required parts exist, validates inventory-to-file mappings, surface-spec address registration, view coverage, address-set equality, home_journey directory placement, and idempotency, then exits non-zero on any failure.

symbols.does: Hashes every file under the blueprint directory for idempotency comparison.
symbols.description: Walks the BLUEPRINT tree and computes SHA-256 hashes keyed by relative paths, returning a dictionary used to compare blueprint content before and after a second emit run.
